proposal_source: idea-engine control/outbox.md @ main 09b38677df0363a7947e1cb2f8b1fb14cbc7fa61 (landed via idea-engine PR #348); idea doc ideas/venture-lab/keyword-tiling-vs-independent-picks-2026-07-13.md @ the same main
harvest_source: menno420/venture-lab docs/publishing/keyword-map.md @ be6c75d4e3379efc108f27d17f2c8ff5adb9a74f (citation only — the runner is fully hermetic, zero repo/network reads)

harvested_sentences_verbatim.header_theory: vetting packets draft "2 categories + 7 keywords" in isolation, so with 14 titles heading for the same storefront several will converge on the same high-value phrases and cannibalize each other's placement
harvested_sentences_verbatim.header_cure: This map makes each packet CLAIM its phrases at vetting time — the way `control/claims/` claims work — so the catalog deliberately tiles the KDP search space instead of piling onto the same shelf
harvested_sentences_verbatim.hard_rule: First claim on `main` wins a collision.
harvested_sentences_verbatim.c1_also_boughts: Two catalog titles occupying the same second node compete with each other for the same also-boughts and category ranking instead of covering two shelves
harvested_sentences_verbatim.c2_revisit_protocol: revisit only if live search results ever show the two listings landing on the same results page

fit_windows.rule: fit = (L - d)/L for d = |k - home| < L else 0

externals.note: shelf carries the first E of the fit pool; externals are never diluted

dilution.rule: j >= 2 catalog claimants of one cell: each effective fit = f/(1 + gamma*(j-1)); externals never diluted

tie_order: externals-first at equal effective fit, then lower title index; TILE claim order t = 1..14

policies.GREEDY: each title independently takes its top-7 keyword / top-2 category cells by SOLO expected traffic (insert own fit among externals-only shelf, externals-first on ties; score = universe_weight * beta_position), ties by lower cell index; collisions allowed
policies.TILE: identical solo scoring; claims in pinned order t = 1..14 restricted to UNCLAIMED cells; on exhaustion the C4 fallback: claim the best already-claimed cell (same score order, excluding own picks); fallback shares counted and reported

metric: R = (T_TILE - T_GREEDY)/T_GREEDY per grid cell, exact Fraction; T = sum_u mass_u * sum_cells weight_cell * catalog click mass on that shelf

decision_bands.approve: R >= 1/50 in >= 9 of 12 cells AND in >= 2 of the 3 gamma=1/4 cells AND the seed-20261322 stability leg reproduces the ruling through the twin evaluators
decision_bands.null: anything else; pre-registered axes: gamma-conditional / overlap-conditional / arm disagreement / sensitivity straddle

arm_s.draw_order: universe coin (keyword w.p. keyword mass) -> phrase index by the universe popularity pmf -> click position by beta; 3 draws per event; SAME draws score both worlds (CRN)

gates: F2 two-title hand world exact values
F3 tie order (external 3/4 above catalog 3/4; claim order 1..14)
F4 beta sums to 1, non-increasing, single-occupant shelf <= 1/2 mass
F5 dilution identities (j=1 -> f; gamma=1,j=2 -> f/2; gamma=4,j=3 -> f/9)
F6 theorem gates (T_TILE gamma-invariant in zero-fallback rows / non-increasing with fallback; T_GREEDY non-increasing in gamma every row; degenerate all-fits-zero -> T = 0)
allocation sanity (exactly 7+2 claims per title both worlds; TILE same-cell pairs only counted fallback shares; GREEDY collisions LOW <= MED <= HIGH expected-direction reporting anomaly flag)
